page.title: Modal Perfect and Deduction
page.subtitle_html: Modal Perfect and Deduction - C1 modal perfect forms for past certainty, possibility, regret, and criticism.
page.subtitle_text: C1 modal perfect forms for past certainty, possibility, regret, and criticism.

blocks.body: {"level":"C1","intro":"Modal Perfect and Deduction: compact anchors for using modal + have + V3 in C1 deduction and evaluation.","rules":[{"label":"Past certainty","text":"Use must have + V3 when evidence strongly points to a past conclusion.","example":"He must have forgotten about the meeting."},{"label":"Past possibility","text":"Use may, might, or could have + V3 when several past explanations are possible.","example":"She might have misunderstood the instructions."},{"label":"Regret or criticism","text":"Use should have, ought to have, or needn't have when judging a past action.","example":"They should have checked the figures earlier."}]}

blocks.heading: C1 focus
blocks.body: This compact page exists as the theory anchor for the English Sentence Builder C1 modal perfect and deduction lesson package.
